solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int strongPasswordChecker(string password) {
        int missing_types = 3;
        if (any_of(password.begin(), password.end(), [](char c) { return islower(c); }))
            missing_types--;
        if (any_of(password.begin(), password.end(), [](char c) { return isupper(c); }))
            missing_types--;
        if (any_of(password.begin(), password.end(), [](char c) { return isdigit(c); }))
            missing_types--;

        int n = password.size();
        int replace = 0;
        int one_count = 0, two_count = 0;
        vector<int> reps;

        for (int i = 0; i < n;) {
            int j = i;
            while (j < n && password[j] == password[i]) j++;
            int len = j - i;
            if (len >= 3) {
                replace += len / 3;
                if (len % 3 == 0) one_count++;
                else if (len % 3 == 1) two_count++;
            }
            reps.push_back(len);
            i = j;
        }

        if (n < 6) return max(6 - n, missing_types);
        else if (n <= 20) {
            int needed = max(replace, missing_types);
            return needed;
        } else {
            int delete_count = n - 20;
            replace -= min(replace, one_count);
            replace -= min(replace, two_count, 2);
            for (int i = 0; i < reps.size(); ++i) {
                if (reps[i] % 3 == 0 && delete_count > 0) {
                    reps[i] -= 1;
                    delete_count--;
                    replace--;
                }
            }
            int to_remove = min(delete_count, 0);
            replace = max(replace, 0);
            int res = delete_count + max(replace, missing_types);
            return res;
        }
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently handles the three constraints of the problem: length, character types, and repeating characters. It calculates the minimum steps required by considering deletions for passwords longer than 20 characters and additions for passwords shorter than 6 characters. It also optimizes replacements to satisfy the repeating character rule. The approach is efficient with linear time complexity and minimal space usage.
